assertions.expectation: Explains that multi-host TPU slices are recreated atomically in a single step — not a rolling upgrade
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Atomic recreation of ALL slices' and 'TPU multislice treats the entire environment as one atomic unit' and contrasts this with 'regular node upgrades where pods migrate gradually'

assertions.expectation: Warns that a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in the multislice environment
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Cascading restart behavior — a maintenance event on ANY single slice triggers restart of ALL slices in your multislice environment'

assertions.expectation: Notes that all pods on all affected slices will restart simultaneously — the training job will be interrupted
assertions.reason: Response states 'All 4 slices restart simultaneously' and 'Complete job termination — your LLM training job will be forcibly stopped'

assertions.expectation: Explains that if insufficient TPU resources are available during recreation, the slice blocks until hardware is freed — potentially causing extended downtime
assertions.reason: Response includes detailed section on capacity risk stating 'if insufficient TPU v5e capacity exists in your zone, the slices will block until hardware becomes available' and mentions 'can be hours if capacity-constrained'

assertions.expectation: Recommends saving a training checkpoint immediately before the maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Force checkpoint immediately before maintenance window' in the checklist and recommends 'Checkpoint aggressively in the 24-48 hours before maintenance'

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring the training framework for automatic checkpoint resume so it can restart after the maintenance event
assertions.reason: Response includes checkpoint restore testing, provides specific restart procedures with 'checkpoint recovery flags', and emphasizes validating checkpoint/restore pipeline

assertions.expectation: Suggests using maintenance exclusions to defer the event if the training run is close to completion
assertions.reason: Response provides specific gcloud command for maintenance exclusions with 'Apply maintenance exclusion to delay (max 30 days)' and complete command syntax

assertions.expectation: Mentions maintenance windows to control when the event occurs — schedule it during a planned break in training
assertions.reason: Response includes 'Control the timing' option with specific gcloud commands for setting maintenance windows and recommends scheduling 'during least critical training phase'
